summaries.Design: Parametric design and recycled materials are enabling more sculptural, sustainable products, while traditional architecture continues inspiring contemporary interpretations of vernacular design.
summaries.Technology: AI is rapidly embedding itself across consumer hardware and platforms—from autonomous phone features to game creation tools—while breakthrough science tackles cancer treatment and atmospheric water extraction.
summaries.Ideas: The AI regulatory landscape is crystallizing with new guidance on psychedelics and data transparency, while labor market shifts reveal hidden costs of loyalty and emerging risks in weather data security.

items.title: ZHA 3D-prints voluptuous chair from recycled plastic
items.snippet: Architecture studio ZHA, formerly Zaha Hadid Architects, has worked with Spanish brand Nagami to launch the 3D-printed Echo Chair made from recycled plastic. The sculptural chair, envisioned using parametric design software, features an arching backrest that extends to the ground in one plane and a
items.line: Parametric design applied to recycled materials demonstrates how computational tools can create sculptural, functional products with lower environmental impact.

items.title: nubia Just Built the World’s First Phone That Does Things on Its Own
items.snippet: nubia Just Built the World’s First Phone That Does Things on Its OwnSmartphone makers have been adding AI features for a few years now, and at this point, the marketing language around them has become fairly predictable....
items.line: A phone that autonomously executes tasks represents a fundamental shift from reactive assistants to proactive AI agents in consumer devices.

items.title: Antibody treatment halts aggressive prostate cancer in early trials
items.snippet: A newly developed antibody has halted the growth and spread of aggressive prostate cancer in preclinical tests, raising hopes for a more targeted way of treating metastatic disease without the side effects that have hampered earlier attempts to block the same cancer-driving pathway.Continue ReadingC
items.line: An antibody treatment halting aggressive prostate cancer growth opens targeted therapeutic pathways that bypass traditional chemotherapy side effects.

items.title: The future of US psychedelic medicine is carved out by the FDA
items.snippet: The US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued its first comprehensive guidance on how psychedelic drugs should best be studied in clinical trials, signalling a shift toward recognizing the potential of these therapeutics. The report also outlines what's needed to see promising compounds appro
items.line: FDA guidance on psychedelic drug trials signals regulatory acceptance of alternative therapeutics and structured pathways to approval.

items.title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
items.snippet: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in many industries, wit
items.line: Weather data sabotage poses systemic risk to airlines, grids, and agriculture, exposing critical infrastructure vulnerability to information manipulation.

items.title: Note Design Studio looks to traditional farmsteads for villa in southern Sweden
items.snippet: Local practice Note Design Studio has completed Villa Österlen, a holiday home in southern Sweden designed as a contemporary take on the traditional rural dwellings of Skåne county. Capable of hosting large family gatherings, the holiday retreat sits within a rolling green landscape close to Hanö Ba
items.line: Contemporary architects drawing from vernacular farmstead design shows how rural building traditions remain relevant to modern luxury hospitality.

items.title: Toad Bakery designed to "avoid the bottleneck of choice paralysis" between pastries
items.snippet: Carpenter Nick Tudor has crafted an elongated, curved counter for the second branch of the popular Toad Bakery in southeast London, in response to its regular winding queues. Toad Bakery is the brainchild of Rebecca Spaven and Oliver Costello, who opened their first site in Camberwell in 2022. Now,
items.line: Solving the physical problem of queue congestion through counter design reveals how spatial UX can address real behavioral friction points.

items.title: Porous compound pulls 2 liters of water from air, and is factory-ready
items.snippet: The Mediterranean is getting hotter and drier, pushing scientists to look for water even in the air itself. A German team has now scaled up a porous material that does exactly that, even when the air feels bone-dry.Continue ReadingCategory: Materials, EngineeringTags: Kiel University, Materials, met
items.line: Scaling a porous compound that extracts water from dry air addresses water scarcity with factory-ready technology.

items.title: Roblox will let people use AI to make games on their phone
items.snippet: Roblox is about to let people make games with AI right inside its mobile app, which could make a platform that's already filled with content of questionable quality feel even more overloaded. The company has embraced AI with open arms, including a preview of an ambitious take on AI world models simi
items.line: Mobile AI-powered game creation democratizes game development but raises questions about content quality on already-saturated platforms.

items.title: The cruel ‘loyalty tax’ blindsiding workers who stayed at their jobs for years
items.snippet: The days of cradle-to-grave jobs are as long gone as fully employer-funded pensions, medical insurance, and annual company picnics for staff and their families. But while most employees have accepted a hefty dose of employment precarity as a fact of today’s labor market, new data shows many longer-t
items.line: Long-tenured employees facing 'loyalty tax' wage penalties reveals a perverse economic incentive structure penalizing workplace stability.

items.title: Studio Font perches cliffside home in Mexico on inhabitable concrete columns
items.snippet: Local practice Studio Font has completed Casa Diez Pies, a home in Mexico that steps down a lush coastal cliff supported in 10 concrete columns. Overlooking the Pacific Ocean in Mazunte, the 250-square-metre home was raised on 10 large concrete columns to lessen its impact on the steeply sloping sit
items.line: Elevating homes on columns to minimize landscape impact demonstrates structural design as an environmental stewardship tool.

items.title: Why heat pumps are still so hot in the US
items.snippet: It feels as if it should be illegal to even think about heating appliances during the height of summer—seriously, these heat waves in New York have been brutal—but we need to talk about heat pumps. The appliances use electricity for heating, they’re incredibly efficient, and they’re on the rise. (Fo
items.line: Heat pumps' efficiency and rising adoption reflect how infrastructure transitions require both technology maturity and sustained policy support.

others.snippet: Giovanni Castellucci, former head of Italy's motorway operator Autostrade per l'Italia, and 31 other people convicted over the 2018 collapse of the Ponte Morandi bridge in Genoa, which killed 43 people.
